assumptions: The developer has a supported browser, email address, Git, npm, and a Unix-capable terminal.
No Plaid team account, Client ID, or Sandbox secret exists yet.
Sandbox first success is acceptable; Production OAuth and paid-plan approval are later gates.

developer_goal: Create a Plaid Dashboard account, run the official Quickstart in Sandbox, create a first Item through Link, and retrieve account data.

first_success.outcome: The Quickstart retrieves account names and balances for a Sandbox Item after Link and token exchange.
first_success.observable_signal: The Quickstart shows /accounts/get data and the guide's Congratulations, you have completed the Plaid Quickstart message.

route_selection.surface: Plaid Dashboard signup plus main web Quickstart repository
route_selection.rule: Prefer Sandbox Quickstart over Production. Do not invent a winner among equal backend language tabs.

candidates.name: Main React Quickstart with Node backend
candidates.reason: Official docs show Node first in several excerpts, but the repo and language tabs also support Python, Ruby, Go, and Java without naming one canonical backend.

candidates.name: Main React Quickstart with Python, Ruby, Go, or Java backend
candidates.reason: Same frontend and terminal outcome, different install and runtime prerequisites; lexicographic action counts cannot be fixed without choosing one.

candidates.name: Postman Collection without cloning Quickstart
candidates.reason: Official Quickstart offers Postman as a no-code alternative that reaches API success on a different interaction surface.

candidates.name: Product-specific quickstarts (Identity Verification, Income, Layer, CRA)
candidates.reason: The main Quickstart page explicitly routes these products to separate starters with different outcomes.

required_path.action: Sign up for the Plaid Dashboard, verify email, and acknowledge required terms.

required_fields.label: Dashboard signup and team profile fields
required_fields.notes: Quickstart requires Dashboard signup; exact current form fields are not enumerated in public docs.

required_path.observable_result: The developer can open Dashboard API Keys and view Client ID and Sandbox secret.

required_path.action: Copy the team Client ID and Sandbox secret from Dashboard API Keys.
required_path.observable_result: Sandbox credentials are available for the Quickstart .env file.

required_path.action: Clone plaid/quickstart, configure .env, install dependencies, and start the selected backend plus React frontend.
required_path.observable_result: The Quickstart is reachable on localhost.

required_path.action: Launch Link, select a Sandbox institution, and complete Sandbox login to create the first Item.
required_path.observable_result: Link returns a public_token and the Quickstart has created an Item.

required_path.action: Use the Quickstart control that calls /accounts/get for the new Item.
required_path.observable_result: Account names and balances are displayed and the guide marks the Quickstart complete.

prerequisites.description: npm and a Unix-capable shell are required for the main Quickstart setup instructions.

external_gates.description: Production access, OAuth institution enablement, and paid pricing plans require separate Dashboard applications and approvals beyond Sandbox.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email verification is required before some Trial and Dashboard flows proceed.

platform_outcomes.description: Plaid issues team Client ID and environment secrets in the Dashboard.
Sandbox Link creates Items and allows /accounts/get without live bank credentials.

excluded.item: Production plan application and OAuth institution registration
excluded.reason: Sandbox Quickstart completes first success without Production entitlement.

excluded.item: Optional OAuth redirect URI registration for localhost
excluded.reason: Quickstart repo documents redirect URI setup as optional for the basic web flow.

sources.title: Sandbox Overview
evidence_supported: Full Sandbox API and Link support

sources.title: Plaid Dashboard signup
evidence_supported: Field-level signup inventory not exposed in public fetch

uncertainties.question: Which backend language defines the canonical Plaid Quickstart path?
uncertainties.impact: Runtime prerequisites, dependency install, and build commands change while the React frontend and outcome stay the same.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Official designation of one default backend, or an explicit corpus policy choosing Node or another language.

uncertainties.question: What exact Plaid signup, team, business, use-case, and anti-abuse screens appear today?
uncertainties.impact: Account-step field counts and possible pre-credential gates remain unverified.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated fresh-account Dashboard signup walkthrough with screenshots.

uncertainties.question: Should Postman count as an equal shortest route to first API success?
uncertainties.impact: Choosing Postman removes clone/run actions and changes the measured surface.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Human judgment on whether the corpus requires the Quickstart app outcome or any Sandbox API success.
